content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all 19 changed source files implementing graceful backend degradation (BackendExhausted). The implementation is well-structured: FailureClass::BackendExhausted is correctly added, retry policy marks it terminal, pattern detection in is_backend_exhausted is thorough with 35+ tests, panel degradation logic is consistent across final_review and completion (with intentional non-degradation for prompt_review), and the engine-level preflight/resume-drift handling is correct. All 754 tests pass. The quorum reduction math is sound — probe-exhausted members are filtered before panel execution, invocation-time exhaustion further reduces effective_min, and all-exhausted scenarios correctly propagate the BackendExhausted error. One minor false-positive concern in the pattern matching; no critical bugs found.

## Amendments

1. In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` around line 1872, the RESET_TIME_INDICATORS entry `"until 20"` (intended to match ISO year prefixes like "until 2026-...") could false-positive on messages like "rate limit reached, until 2000 requests are processed" or "until 200 more attempts". Consider tightening to `"until 20"` followed by a digit check (e.g. matching `until 20\d\d-` for ISO dates) or using a regex, to avoid misclassifying retryable messages as BackendExhausted.
   Rationale: While unlikely in practice (backends rarely phrase messages this way), the current substring match is too broad. A false positive here would mark a retryable rate-limit as terminal BackendExhausted, permanently removing the backend from the panel for the rest of the run. The existing test suite covers "until 5 retries" and "until 100 requests" (which don't match "until 20"), but misses "until 2000 requests" which would incorrectly trigger.
